summary: Iteration 22 - Tier/Price Consistency Testing. The fix was PARTIALLY applied. Autocomplete endpoint correctly uses get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() and returns correct tier/price. However, the /api/celebrity/search endpoint still uses stale DB values for existing celebrities, causing tier/price mismatch.

critical.issue: Returns incorrect tier/price for existing DB celebrities. Pink (singer) returns D-list/£0.6M instead of A-list/£12M. Daniel Radcliffe returns B-list/£6M instead of A-list/£12M.
critical.root_cause: Lines 3702-3712 in server.py use stored DB values (existing.get('tier'), existing.get('price')) instead of recalculating using get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata()

passed_tests: GET /api/autocomplete - Tier/price consistency for Taylor Swift, Ed Sheeran, Tom Holland, Adele
GET /api/hot-celebs - Returns 13 celebrities with consistent tier/price
Hot celebs tier/price consistency - All 13 celebrities have matching base prices for their tiers
Pink alias mapping - Searching 'pink' returns Pink (singer) in autocomplete
Frontend loads correctly with Hot Celebs banner

failed_tests.reason: DB has stale tier/price, endpoint doesn't recalculate
DB has stale tier/price, endpoint doesn't recalculate

action_items: FIX REQUIRED: In /api/celebrity/search endpoint (server.py lines 3702-3712), replace the code that uses stored DB tier/price with a call to get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() for existing celebrities
The fix should be similar to what was done in the autocomplete endpoint - use the unified tier/price calculation function
Consider also updating the DB records with correct tier/price after recalculation to avoid repeated API calls
critical_code_review_comments: The fix was applied to /api/autocomplete endpoint but NOT to /api/celebrity/search endpoint
Lines 3702-3712 in server.py still use existing.get('tier', 'D') and existing.get('price', 3.0) for DB-cached celebrities
This causes inconsistency: autocomplete shows correct tier/price, but clicking to add shows incorrect values
DB has stale data: Pink (singer) stored as D-list/£0.6M, Daniel Radcliffe stored as B-list/£6M despite having 100 language count
context_for_next_testing_agent: The tier/price consistency fix was PARTIALLY applied. Autocomplete endpoint works correctly. The /api/celebrity/search endpoint needs the same fix applied - it should use get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() instead of stored DB values for existing celebrities. Test cases are in test_iteration_22_tier_price_consistency.py.

rca_of_the_issue.issue: Tier/price mismatch between autocomplete and celebrity/search endpoints
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The fix to use get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() was applied to autocomplete endpoint but not to celebrity/search endpoint. The celebrity/search endpoint (lines 3702-3712) still uses stored DB values which are stale/incorrect.
reproduction_steps: 1. Search for 'Daniel Radcliffe' in autocomplete - shows A-list, £12M (correct)
2. Click on Daniel Radcliffe to view details via /api/celebrity/search - shows B-list, £6M (incorrect)
3. Same issue with Pink (singer) - autocomplete shows A-list, search shows D-list
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Apply the same fix to /api/celebrity/search endpoint: replace lines 3702-3712 with a call to get_tier_and_price_from_wikidata() to recalculate tier/price for existing DB celebrities
